ideation: This sequence simulates the action of driving a speedboat, focusing on the arm movements involved in gripping the wheel and controlling the throttle.  The animation will emphasize dynamic movement and a sense of speed and control. Frame 1: Both arms are extended forward, hands gripping an imaginary wheel.  The wrists will be slightly angled downward, suggesting a firm grip. Frame 2: The left arm pulls back slightly, mimicking the action of adjusting the throttle. The right arm remains firmly on the wheel. Frame 3: Both arms simultaneously push forward, conveying acceleration. Frame 4: The left arm returns to its initial position, maintaining balance. Frame 5: Both arms return to a neutral position, as if the boat is slowing down.  The overall motion should convey a sense of speed and control, suggestive of the excitement of operating a speedboat.

frames.beat: arms grip the wheel, ready to speed off
left arm pulls back, adjusting the throttle slightly
both arms push forward, accelerating the boat
left arm returns, maintaining control and balance
arms relax as the boat slows to a stop
